provenance.title: Attention-based dynamic multilayer graph neural networks for loan default prediction

authors.name: Sahab Zandi
authors.affiliation: Department of Statistical and Actuarial Sciences, Western University, Canada

authors.name: Kamesh Korangi
authors.affiliation: Department of Decision Analytics and Risk, University of Southampton, UK

authors.name: María Óskarsdóttir
authors.affiliation: Department of Computer Science, Reykjavík University, Iceland

authors.name: Christophe Mues
authors.affiliation: Department of Decision Analytics and Risk, University of Southampton, UK

authors.name: Cristián Bravo
authors.affiliation: Department of Statistical and Actuarial Sciences, Western University, Canada

provenance.abstract: We present a model for credit risk assessment leveraging a dynamic multilayer network built from a Graph Neural Network and a Recurrent Neural Network, each layer reflecting a different source of network connection. We test our methodology using a dataset provided by U.S. mortgage financier Freddie Mac.
key_claims: DYMGNN is a novel approach for node classification in multilayer networks, combining GNN+RNN+optional attention
GAT-LSTM-ATT on double-layer network achieves the best AUC (0.812) and F1 (0.851) among all configurations
Dynamic multilayer model outperforms static GNN baselines, XGBoost, Logistic Regression, and DNN
Custom soft attention mechanism over time snapshots consistently improves performance vs. without attention
Most recent snapshots receive the highest attention weight; longer lookback periods yield diminishing returns

architecture.description: Dynamic Multilayer GNN (DYMGNN). Two main families: GNN-RNN (without attention) and GNN-RNN-ATT (with temporal attention). GNN layer is either GCN or GAT; RNN is either LSTM or GRU. Best configuration is GAT-LSTM-ATT on double-layer network.

components.name: SupraAdjacencyMatrix
components.description: Block matrix A ∈ R^{nl × nl} encoding intra-layer and inter-layer connections. For l=2 layers (area + company), n=nodes per layer, total size (2n × 2n).

components.name: GCNLayer
components.description: Isotropic spectral GCN. Z = D̃^{-1/2} Ã D̃^{-1/2} X W^T. Ã = A + I_{nl} (self-loops added). Applied to each snapshot G^(t) independently.

components.name: GATLayer
components.description: Multi-head attention graph convolution. Computes attention coefficients e_ij via LeakyReLU, normalizes with softmax, aggregates neighbour embeddings weighted by alpha_ij. Best performing topological component.

components.name: LSTMLayer
components.description: Standard LSTM processing sequence of GNN embeddings [Z^(1)_i, ..., Z^(t)_i] for each node i. 3 gates: input, forget, output. H^(t) ∈ R^{nl×D}, C^(t) ∈ R^{nl×D}.

components.name: GRULayer
components.description: GRU with update and reset gates as alternative to LSTM. H^(t) ∈ R^{nl×D}.

components.name: TemporalAttention
components.description: Computes scalar attention score s^(t) = a_h H^(t) W_h, normalizes with softmax over τ timestamps to get β^(t), then H_att = Σ β^(t) H^(t). Re-weights snapshot hidden states by importance. Only in GNN-RNN-ATT family.

components.name: Decoder
components.description: Applied to node embeddings H^(τ) (no attention) or H_att (with attention). Architecture from Fig. 5: Dense(20,10) → ReLU → Dropout(0.5) → Dense(10,1) → Sigmoid.
components.note: Fig. 5 shows Dense(20,10) → ReLU → Dropout(0.5) → Dense(10,1) → Sigmoid. Intermediate hidden sizes explicitly given.

components.name: NodeDropout
components.description: 50% of nodes randomly isolated (no connections) in each snapshot of a window. Applied during training only.

network_types.single_layer_area: Nodes connected if first two digits of zip code match
network_types.single_layer_company: Nodes connected if they share the same mortgage provider
network_types.double_layer_area_company: Both layers combined in supra adjacency matrix; interlayer edges connect identical nodes across layers

equations.name: GCN Layer Forward Pass
equations.description: Ã = A + I_{nl} (adjacency with self-loops), D̃ diagonal degree matrix of Ã, W^T ∈ R^{d×D} learnable weight matrix, D = embedding size.

equations.name: GAT Unnormalized Attention Score
equations.description: ‖ = concatenation, a^T ∈ R^{1×2D} learnable weight vector, W ∈ R^{D×d} weight matrix.

equations.name: GAT Softmax Normalization
equations.description: N(v_i) = neighbourhood of node v_i, self-loop included.

equations.name: GAT Neighbourhood Aggregation
equations.description: Single-head formulation. Multi-head output aggregated by averaging.

equations.name: Temporal Attention Score (unnormalized)
equations.description: a_h ∈ R^{1×nl}, W_h ∈ R^{D×1} are learnable weight vectors.

tensors.name: X_t
tensors.note: Node feature matrix at snapshot t. n=nodes per layer, l=layers, d=16 features (Table 1)

tensors.name: A_t
tensors.note: Supra adjacency matrix at snapshot t

tensors.name: Z_t
tensors.note: GNN embedding at snapshot t. D = embedding dimension (not stated, assumed 64)

tensors.name: H_t
tensors.note: RNN hidden state at snapshot t

tensors.name: C_t
tensors.note: LSTM cell state at snapshot t

tensors.name: s_t
tensors.note: Temporal attention score for snapshot t

tensors.name: beta_t
tensors.note: Normalized temporal attention weight for snapshot t

tensors.name: Y_hat
tensors.note: Predicted default probability for each node

training_pipeline.framework: PyTorch (implied by deep learning context; not explicitly stated)

node_dropout.description: 50% of nodes randomly isolated in each snapshot

embedding_dim_D.basis: common default for GNN credit scoring tasks

gat_num_heads.basis: common default for GAT; paper mentions multi-head averaging

evaluation_protocol.task: Binary node classification — loan default prediction (90+ days arrears within next 12 months)

dataset.name: Freddie Mac Single-Family Loan-Level (SFLL) Dataset
dataset.class_imbalance: minority=default: 7426 of 148520 total (5.0% default rate approx.)

interpretability.attention_analysis: Attention weights increase sharply from timestamp 3 to 6; peak at t=6 (~0.6)

implementation_assumptions.basis: Not stated in paper. Common default for GNN credit-scoring tasks of this size.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: GAT uses 4 attention heads with average aggregation
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper mentions multi-head GAT with averaging. 4 is the most common default in GAT literature.
Deep learning paper; PyTorch is standard for custom GNN research; no framework stated.
Paper does not mention stacking. Single layer is the default for sequence-to-sequence tasks on medium-size data.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Decoder hidden sizes: D→20→10→1 (from Fig. 5 label 'Dense Layer (20, 10)' and 'Dense Layer (10, 1)')
implementation_assumptions.basis: Fig. 5 explicitly labels layer sizes. Input to first Dense layer is embedding dim D.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: GCN uses a single layer (not stacked multi-hop)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Equations 1-4 describe single-layer operations. Paper does not mention stacking.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Node features are normalized per-snapshot using the global min-max from training data
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says min-max scaling is used. Global scaling avoids train-test leakage.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: H^(0) and C^(0) initialized to zeros (not learned)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says 'can be initialized with zeros or learned'; zeros is the simpler common default.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Batch training over all nodes in a snapshot window simultaneously (full-batch GNN)
implementation_assumptions.basis: GNN credit scoring typically uses full-batch since graph connectivity matters. No mini-batch GNN mentioned.

ambiguities.description: Number of GAT attention heads H not specified. Paper says 'multiple attention heads' with averaging.
GNN embedding dimension D not specified anywhere in the paper.
Validation set construction not described. Baselines are tuned on a 'validation dataset' but its size/construction is not given for the GNN models.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: A portion of training windows used for validation
alternatives: Last training window used as validation

ambiguities.description: Node dropout (50% isolation) — whether applied at training time only or also at test time.
